steps.title: Recall the tangent ratio
steps.content: In a right triangle, $\tan A = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}$ — the side opposite angle $A$ over the side adjacent to angle $A$ (not the hypotenuse).

steps.title: Plug in the side lengths
steps.content:
Triangle ABC has a right angle at B, hypotenuse AC = 114, and angle C = 30°. Since the angles of a triangle sum to 180°, angle A = 180° − 90° − 30° = 60°.
In a 30-60-90 triangle with hypotenuse 114: the side opposite 30° (AB) = 114 ÷ 2 = 57, and the side opposite 60° (BC) = 57√3.
24994 an A = \dfrac{ ext{opposite}}{ ext{adjacent}} = \dfrac{BC}{AB} = \dfrac{57\sqrt{3}}{57} = \sqrt{3} pprox 1.73224994
The answer is 1.732.
